headings.text: Begin your retreat — twenty-four suites, infinite horizon.

paragraphs.text: Twenty-four private suites carved into volcanic stone, where the Aegean opens beneath a sky of burnished gold and the sea remembers your name.
Three pillars define the Obsidian Cove experience — each crafted for guests who measure luxury not in marble, but in solitude, salt air, and the slow turning of the sun.
Perched 80 metres above the Aegean on a black volcanic promontory, every window frames an unbroken horizon — sea, sky, and the distant lanterns of Naxos at dusk.
Each suite is paired with a heated obsidian-edged plunge pool, set on its own terrace and shielded from neighbouring views — yours alone, from sunrise to last light.
Petra Grotto is carved directly into the cliff face — a candlelit cavern where Aegean mezze, wood-fired fish and natural wines arrive at tables touched by sea-spray.

Obsidian Cove was conceived as the antidote to the modern resort — no lobbies, no lines, no rush. Twenty-four suites, twelve metres of cliff between each, and a single sweeping path down to the water.
Our staff outnumber guests two to one. Our chef sources from six island farms within twenty kilometres. And every morning, the sea is waiting.
We arrived expecting a hotel. We left having discovered a kind of stillness I did not know existed — a place where the sea does the speaking and one's own thoughts return, softer, like tide.
A clifftop retreat on the Aegean coast. Twenty-four private suites, a sea-cave dining grotto, and curated coastal experiences — open year round.
